_meta.description: Usul (foundational rules) of the 20 canonical riwayat from the 10 Qira'at
sources: An-Nashr fi al-Qira'at al-'Ashr by Ibn al-Jazari (via islamweb.net)

featureKeys.imalah: none = no tilting | partial = taqlil/bayna bayna | heavy = imalah kubra
featureKeys.madd: short = 2 harakaat (qasr) | medium = 4 harakaat (tawassut) | long = 6 harakaat (ishba')
featureKeys.basmalah: always = basmalah between every two surahs | not_always = has wasl/sakt options
featureKeys.hamzah: clear = tahqiq, all hamzahs pronounced | softened = tashil/ibdal/naql
featureKeys.takbir: yes = takbir between surahs in Juz 'Amma | no = no takbir
featureKeys.sirat: sad = pure Sad | sin = Sin | ishmam = Sad mixed with Zay
featureKeys.ra: standard = tafkhim in normal positions | thin = tarqiq in extra positions (Warsh)
featureKeys.extraYa: yes = additional Ya' at end of certain words | no = standard
featureKeys.imalahScope: none = no imalah | standard = common positions | broad = extended to extra words
featureKeys.idghamKabir: yes = merging identical/similar letters across word boundaries | no = no idgham kabir
featureKeys.haKinayah: standard = normal treatment | with_sila = connecting vowel added to pronoun Ha'

riwayat.name: Hafs 'an 'Asim
riwayat.region: Most of the Muslim world — Saudi Arabia, Egypt, South/Southeast Asia, Turkey
riwayat.verifiedSource: nquran.com/ar/view/16696 (Shu'bah vs Hafs comparison) + nquran.com/ar/view/16701 (Hafs from Tayyibah)
riwayat.notes: The most widely used riwayah globally. Madd munfasil = 2 harakaat (4 via some Tayyibah turuq).

riwayat.name: Warsh 'an Nafi'
riwayat.region: North Africa — Morocco, Algeria, parts of Libya, West Africa
riwayat.notes: Basmalah has 3 options: basmalah, sakt, wasl. Madd munfasil/muttasil = 6 harakaat (ishba'). Taqlil on ya-origin alifs. Extensive naql. Ra' tarqiq when preceded by ya sakinah or kasrah.

riwayat.name: Qalun 'an Nafi'
riwayat.notes: Madd munfasil = qasr (2) or tawassut (4). No imalah except هار with imalah kubra. Silat mim al-jam' optional. Has tashil on double hamzahs (same-word: tashil 2nd with idkhal; cross-word: drops 1st or tashil), but single hamzahs are mostly tahqiq.

riwayat.name: Al-Duri 'an Abu 'Amr
riwayat.region: Sudan, parts of West Africa, Somalia
riwayat.notes: Basmalah has 3 options. Madd munfasil = 4 harakaat. No idgham kabir (reads with clarity per nquran 16698 — idgham kabir is Susi's distinction). Imalah on feminine alifs, after ra', in 11 surahs' final verses.

riwayat.name: Al-Susi 'an Abu 'Amr
riwayat.notes: Extensive idgham kabir (identical + similar letters). Ibdal of quiescent hamzahs. More extensive imalah application than Duri in some positions. Per nquran comparison: Susi does idgham while Duri reads with idhar.

riwayat.name: Khalaf 'an Hamzah
riwayat.verifiedSource: nquran.com/ar/view/16678 (Khallad page, shared Hamzah usul) + An-Nashr imalah chapter
riwayat.notes: Ishmam on al-Sirat. Sakt (pause) on consonant before hamzah — key distinction from Khallad. Basmalah: wasl only (no basmalah between surahs). Confirmed via An-Nashr: heavy imalah on all ya-origin alifs.

riwayat.name: Khallad 'an Hamzah
riwayat.notes: Ishmam on al-Sirat in Umm al-Kitab (Fatihah) only, sad elsewhere. Both Khalaf and Khallad share ishmam in Fatihah. No sakt before hamzah (unlike Khalaf). Comprehensive idgham kabir. Madd = 6 harakaat (ishba').

riwayat.name: Abu al-Harith 'an Al-Kisa'i
riwayat.verifiedSource: nquran.com/ar/view/16694 (Abu al-Harith vs Duri comparison) + An-Nashr imalah chapter
riwayat.notes: Al-Kisa'i's imalah confirmed via An-Nashr (heavy on all ya-origin alifs + additional words not shared with Hamzah). Abu al-Harith has standard scope of imalah within Al-Kisa'i's system.

riwayat.name: Al-Duri 'an Al-Kisa'i
riwayat.verifiedSource: nquran.com/ar/view/16694 (Abu al-Harith vs Duri comparison) + An-Nashr imalah chapter
riwayat.notes: Same Duri who narrates from Abu 'Amr. Broader imalah scope than Abu al-Harith — extends to additional words and positions.

riwayat.name: Hisham 'an Ibn 'Amir
riwayat.notes: Tashil on some double-hamzahs. Idgham of dhal (idh) and dal (qad) into following letters. Selective imalah on specific words per Tayyibah (less extensive than Ibn Dhakwan). Basmalah has options (shared qari rule with Ibn Dhakwan). Per nquran comparison: Hisham has idgham where Ibn Dhakwan has idhar.

riwayat.name: Ibn Dhakwan 'an Ibn 'Amir
riwayat.notes: Basmalah has 3 options per Tayyibah. Has imalah kubra on limited positions via al-Suri tariq (specific words with final alif-raa, certain past-tense verbs), but not systematic enough to be audibly distinctive. Madd munfasil = 4 harakaat.

riwayat.name: Al-Bazzi 'an Ibn Kathir
riwayat.notes: Takbir between surahs from Al-Duha to Al-Nas. Madd munfasil = qasr (2 harakaat) per An-Nashr and scholarly consensus. Distinctive hamzah handling in some words (idkhal between two hamzahs).

riwayat.name: Qunbul 'an Ibn Kathir
riwayat.notes: al-Sirat with Sin. No takbir (only Bazzi has takbir per nquran). Madd munfasil = qasr (2 harakaat), same as Bazzi. Connects hamzahs in certain double-hamzah constructions.

riwayat.name: Shu'bah 'an 'Asim
riwayat.notes: Same Qari' as Hafs but very different transmission. Madd munfasil = 4 harakaat. Different word choices in several ayat vs Hafs.

riwayat.name: Ibn Wardan 'an Abu Ja'far
riwayat.notes: nquran confirms: basmalah always, no takbir, qasr on madd munfasil, extensive hamzah tashil. Ibn Wardan has sila on ha' al-kinayah in more positions than Ibn Jammaz.

riwayat.name: Ibn Jammaz 'an Abu Ja'far
riwayat.notes: Shares Abu Ja'far's general usul. Standard ha' al-kinayah treatment (no extra sila).

riwayat.name: Ya'qub al-Hadrami (Ruways)
riwayat.notes: Idgham kabir in specific positions. Hamzah tashil on second of doubles + naql. Additional Ya' on certain words. Ha' al-kinayah with sila. Per nquran comparison: Ruways does idgham while Rawh reads with clarity.

riwayat.name: Ya'qub al-Hadrami (Rawh)
riwayat.notes: Shares Ya'qub's extra Ya' feature. Standard ha' al-kinayah (no extra sila). Fewer ishmam cases than Ruways.

riwayat.name: Khalaf al-'Ashir (Ishaq)
riwayat.notes: nquran confirms: wasl only (no basmalah), no takbir, tawassut madd, heavy imalah, extensive idgham. The 10th Qari' — his own reading is distinct from his narration of Hamzah.

riwayat.name: Khalaf al-'Ashir (Idris)
riwayat.notes: Same Qari' as Ishaq but no idgham kabir. Distinguished from Ishaq by this feature.
